pull_request.title: chore(java): Remove Java-based OpenToken CLI in favor of Python CLI
pull_request.body:
## Summary

- Remove the deleted Java CLI module and make the Python CLI the only supported CLI surface.
- Clean up follow-on wiring so docs, demos, release tooling, and interoperability checks all match the Python-only CLI model.

## Related issues

- Fixes #266
- Related to #208
- Related to #238

## Affected surfaces

- [x] Java
- [x] Python
- [x] CLI
- [ ] PySpark
- [x] Docs / GitHub Pages
- [x] CI / workflows / agent instructions
- [x] Release process

## What changed

- Delete `lib/java/opentoken-cli` and remove module, build, and release wiring that only existed for the Java CLI.
- Preserve Java/Python parity by adding a Java core-library interoperability harness and comparing it against the Python CLI.
- Update workflows, sync tooling, version bump targets, demos, and docs/pages to point to the Python CLI as the supported CLI surface.
- Remove stale Java CLI examples from the Java API docs and clarify that Java remains a library API rather than a file-processing CLI.
- Mitigate branch follow-up issues by fixing stale Java CLI references, broken interoperability README commands, and missing bumpversion coverage for Python `pyproject.toml` files.

## Testing

- [x] `python tools/interoperability/cli_parity_test.py`
- [x] `python tools/interoperability/multi_language_interoperability_test.py`
- [x] `PYTHONPATH=lib/python/opentoken/src/main:lib/python/opentoken-cli/src/main pytest lib/python/opentoken-cli/src/test -q`
- [x] `cd lib/java && mvn -q test`
- [x] `bash -n demos/pprl-superhero-example/run_end_to_end.sh`
- [x] `git diff --check`

+---
+applyTo: "**"
+description: "Always run pre-commit hooks after making code changes"
+---
+
+# Pre-Commit Hook Enforcement
+
+After making any code changes, always run pre-commit hooks before considering the task complete:
+
+```bash
+prek run --files <changed-files>
+```
+
+Or to run against all staged changes:
+
+```bash
+prek run
+```
+
+If prek reports any failures (formatting, linting, type errors), fix them and re-run until all hooks pass. Do not mark work as done while prek hooks are failing.

 ### Security Best Practices <!-- omit in toc -->
 
 1. **Key Management**:
+
    - Use strong, cryptographically random keys (not the demo keys!)
    - Share keys only through secure channels (encrypted email, key management systems)
    - Rotate keys periodically (requires re-tokenization)
 
 2. **Access Control**:
+
    - Limit decryption capability to authorized matching systems only
    - Log all decryption and matching operations
    - Use separate environments for tokenization vs. matching
 
 3. **Data Governance**:
+
    - Establish data use agreements before sharing tokens
    - Define permitted uses of matching results
    - Implement audit trails for all token operations

commit_summaries.message:
Remove Java CLI module and update build/CI/tooling references

Co-authored-by: mattwise-42 <106715847+mattwise-42@users.noreply.github.com>

Update all documentation and tooling to remove Java CLI references

Co-authored-by: mattwise-42 <106715847+mattwise-42@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix interoperability test CI failure and remove Java dependency from interop job

Co-authored-by: mattwise-42 <106715847+mattwise-42@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Java library parity harness for interoperability tests

Co-authored-by: mattwise-42 <106715847+mattwise-42@users.noreply.github.com>
refactor: update Dockerfile for Python CLI and simplify command banner logic

Refactor OpenToken to remove Java CLI dependency and transition to Python CLI

- Updated .bumpversion.cfg to reflect changes in versioning for Python files.
- Modified PPRL_Superhero_Demo.ipynb to clarify fallback to Python CLI.
- Revised README.md to remove Java prerequisites and emphasize Python CLI usage.
- Adjusted run_end_to_end.sh to remove Java build steps and focus on Python CLI.
- Updated development guide to reflect the removal of Java CLI and emphasize Python setup.
- Revised Java API documentation to remove references to I/O classes and focus on in-memory token generation.
- Updated interoperability tests to reflect changes in the Python CLI and added new metadata checks.
feat: add pre-commit hook instructions for code changes enforcement
